steps.title: Solve the linear equation for y
Substitute into the quadratic and solve for x
steps.content:
Substitute $y = x + 32$ into $y = x^2 - 30x$:
$$x + 32 = x^2 - 30x$$

Rearrange:
$$x^2 - 31x - 32 = 0$$

Factor by finding two numbers that multiply to $-32$ and add to $-31$:
$$(x - 32)(x + 1) = 0$$

So $x = 32$ or $x = -1$.

steps.title: Back-substitute to find y-values
steps.content:
Using $y = x + 32$:
  • When $x = 32$: $y = 32 + 32 = 64$
  • When $x = -1$: $y = -1 + 32 = 31$

The possible values are $y = 64$ or $y = 31$. Since 64 appears in the answer choices, that matches choice D — D is correct.
